samples.xml_description:
Implement a 16-bit divider module, the dividend is 16-bit and the divider is 8-bit in combinational logic. Extract the higher bits of the dividend, matching the bit width of the divisor. Compare these bits with the divisor: if the dividend bits are greater, set the quotient to 1, otherwise set it to 0, and use the difference as the remainder. Concatenate the remainder with the highest remaining 1-bit of the dividend, and repeat the process until all dividend bits are processed. 16-bit dividend. 8-bit divisor. 16-bit quotient. 16-bit remainder. The parameter i represents the index of the current bit of dividend being processed, ranging from 0 to 8. The parameter j represents the index of the current bit of divisor being processed, ranging from 0 to 7. The parameter n represents the current bit of dividend being processed, ranging from 0 to 15. The parameter m represents the current bit of divisor being processed, ranging from 0 to 7. The parameter k represents the current bit of remainder being processed, ranging from 0 to 15. The parameter l represents the current bit of quotient being processed, ranging from 0 to 8.

1. Initialize the registers a_reg and b_reg to 0.

2. Always block triggered by changes in inputs A and B:

2.1 If the higher bits of a_reg (bits 15 to 8) are greater than or equal to B, subtract B from those bits and set the quotient bit (l) to 1.

2.2 If the higher bits of a_reg are less than B, do not change the quotient and keep the difference as the remainder.

2.3 Shift the remainder left by one bit and append the next bit of a_reg (n) to the least significant bit.

3. Reset the registers a_reg and b_reg with the values of A and B.
